Team -- Like so many of us, Justice Sonia Sotomayor says she was shocked and saddened when the Supreme Court's far-right majority overturned _Roe v. Wade._

**But she isn’t giving up.** Justice Sotomayor says she'll keep fighting for us every single day, and we MUST follow her example!

We’ve launched an URGENT petition to show that Democrats won’t back down. We’ll ALWAYS fight for reproductive freedoms. And with Republicans doing everything they can to take away our rights, _now is the time to step up._ **Let's stand with Justice Sotomayor TODAY:**

name: Glenn Grothman
subject: Happy Mother's Day!
body:
| | | [](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=f9ef163c38f94024905cc6b5e55f222e&_m=1e10368b174b4573b8a3d0da1a3d734d&_e=sZG80akDvKMUk_0_xYbHz536x2DRChAzWuZgPjeRKshG7vFp4TvBxncfO5Fm8OQ15V-AloDU2P6Zlunqdki28EFgkfDT5ltgfsCgg1KScQw3uCc7-cbKADz31EnxrfwV258-J9-qZpubRFn-866HqkXhpKxYLTiXJ1urqnIybge3jhpj8jlqVPuXZ2kb4sB5DC2UOXszeJnzb8KuNlACuw%3D%3D)
---
Happy Mother's Day, my friend! President Abraham Lincoln put it best when he said, "All that I am, or hope to be, I owe to my angel mother." I'm proud to celebrate mothers across our great country for the love and support they give us. I wouldn't be where I am today if it wasn't for my mom and the love she's given me my whole life. _**Happy Mother's Day, and may you have a wonderful day with your family! **_ Thank you and God Bless, **Glenn Grothman**
---
|

clean_body: | | | Happy Mother's Day, my friend! President Abraham Lincoln put it best when he said, "All that I am, or hope to be, I owe to my angel mother." I'm proud to celebrate mothers across our great country for the love and support they give us. I wouldn't be where I am today if it wasn't for my mom and the love she's given me my whole life. Happy Mother's Day, and may you have a wonderful day with your family! Thank you and God Bless, Glenn Grothman | | | | | Follow Glenn Grothman on Social Media! | | | ---|---|--- | | Glenn Grothman for Congress P.O. Box 1215 Fond du Lac, WI 54936 You are receiving this email because you expressed an interest in Glenn Grothman.

name: Callie Barr
subject: Happy Mother’s Day! What Congress needs? More moms.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Callie Barr for Congress
body:
Do you think our government would be such a mess if more moms were in charge? Not a chance - moms know that our kids’ future depends on a functioning government. | Peter, I just want to take a moment to wish all our moms, grandmas, aunts, and mother figures out there a **Happy Mother’s Day!** Michigan moms – we have grit. If you want a job done right, you call us. And, we pass down that same grit and work ethic to our kids as they grow up right here in Northern Michigan and the Upper Peninsula. | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63279417/406218978/-621152178?refcode=fl_em_na_20230514mday_cb_full_xx_img1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTRhNDRjZmYtNjdmMi1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=EauOUGWBLrz7LH1mIIsRd9Ni9fIM2UwQu3b0potfRhk=)
---
I know times are tough right now for my fellow moms. The pandemic changed our families in ways we never could have imagined. The cost of child care is out of reach for many, leaving many parents struggling between working and paying for child care. It's why I tell folks that so many moms simply can't _afford_ to work.
I have a thought on why Congress isn't doing right by moms: Only 7% of our representatives are moms with kids under 18 years old. **Do you think Congress would be such a mess if more moms were in charge? Not a chance – moms know that our kids’ future depends on functioning leadership.**
So on this Mother’s Day, let's celebrate all moms and mom figures – and make sure we're electing leaders who will have our backs.
Thank you,
Callie Barr
Candidate
Michigan’s 1st Congressional District

| Callie Barr is a 5th-generation Michigander, advocate for veterans, and lawyer. She’s running to take on extremism and do-nothing politicians in D.C. that are leaving rural America behind. Can you help send Callie to Congress by donating today?

name: Election Alert via Courier Newsroom
subject: Why we ALL have to pay attention to this Virginia election
body:
We’re coming up on some of the most important state elections of 2023: the Virginia state primaries.
# [](https://act.couriernewsroom.com/go/83?t=1&akid=958%2E120351%2EyFOf4B)
Peter — we’re coming up on some of the most important state elections of 2023: **the Virginia state primaries.**
[**We’ll explain more about the consequences of this election in a moment, but if you’re short on time: will you chip in $5 now to help us reach our $7,000 mid-month goal so we can mobilize Virginia voters and spread accurate information about the candidates?**](https://act.couriernewsroom.com/go/3706?refcode=em-20230514&t=2&akid=958%2E120351%2EyFOf4B)
Virginia is one of a handful of states holding major elections this year, with all 140 members of their General Assembly up for reelection.
With Republicans holding a narrow majority in the House of Delegates, and Democrats with a tight lead in the State Senate, every single race can have a big impact on which party ends up in control of the state government — **and its policy agenda.**
Virginia Republicans have been ramping up their attacks on public education and abortion rights. Their actions set a serious, grave precedent for state and local governments across the country, **which is exactly why this primary election will impact all of us.**
Knowing that, our reporters at _Dogwood_ have jumped right into covering **everything Virginians need to know to vote in next month’s primary election** — from when the voter registration deadline is to all the ways they can cast their ballot:
[](https://act.couriernewsroom.com/go/3706?refcode=em-20230514&t=3&akid=958%2E120351%2EyFOf4B)
The work of our _Dogwood_ team — the articles, the amplified posts, and the ad placements — is only possible with support from people like you. **That’s why we’ve set a $7,000 mid-month goal to ensure they have all the resources they need ahead of next month’s election.**
[**Will you chip in $5 now to power their work and help us spread accurate, factual information to as many voters as possible?**](https://act.couriernewsroom.com/go/3706?refcode=em-20230514&t=4&akid=958%2E120351%2EyFOf4B)
**[Chip in $5 »](https://act.couriernewsroom.com/go/3706?refcode=em-20230514&t=5&akid=958%2E120351%2EyFOf4B)**
Thanks for your support,
Everyone at Courier Newsroom
**[DONATE »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-footer?refcode=em-footer&t=6&refcode2=958_120351_yFOf4B&akid=958%2E120351%2EyFOf4B)**
Email us: [**info@couriernewsroom.com**](mailto:info@couriernewsroom.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.couriernewsroom.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.couriernewsroom.com/signup/update-your-information)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.couriernewsroom.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=7&akid=958%2E120351%2EyFOf4B)

To give by check:
Courier Newsroom
Box 509
New York, NY 10032

body:
from MAGA extremists like MTG

| | Peter,

Marjorie Taylor Greene’s time in Congress has been marked by one embarrassing, disqualifying scandal after another.

Antisemitic conspiracy theories. COVID vaccine denialism. Shouting and jeering at Joe Biden during his State of the Union. Accusing the entire Biden family of sex trafficking. And undermining our democracy by denying the 2020 election results, cheering the Jan. 6 insurrection, **and supporting MAGA GOP election rigging!**

The best time to stand up and fight back against MTG was before she got into office. The second best time is now. **That’s why we’re launching a critical mid-month donor drive to raise the resources we need to stomp out MTG’s MAGA movement once and for all.**

[**_Chip in now to stop Marjorie Taylor Greene >>>_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63279591/406219591/-1313339816?refcode=20230514.1_SMTG_5AB&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZWNhMzUxZS02OGYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aEYV4wh70IlHsfs7NmaAp4wdO1xjDvXQrgNGKLs7bYk=)

Here’s why this donor drive is so important. Georgia is MTG’s home, and it’s _**the most important swing state!**_

If we keep Georgia blue, Joe Biden _**will be re-elected.**_ And since MTG is a potential VP pick for Donald Trump, we need to keep Georgia blue to keep MTG from getting within a heartbeat of the presidency.

If we keep the White House and flip the House, **we can boot MTG off some of the most powerful committees in the House and show her the door.**

[**_But all of this comes down to you–our grassroots team–and whether we can marshal the resources we need right now to fund critical organizing efforts in Georgia. And that’s why we’re having this mid-month donor drive. Please, can you rush a donation to help us defeat MTG’s toxic MAGA extremism?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63279593/406219593/-1313339816?refcode=20230514.1_SMTG_5AB&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlZWNhMzUxZS02OGYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aEYV4wh70IlHsfs7NmaAp4wdO1xjDvXQrgNGKLs7bYk=)

name: Gabe Amo
subject: 🌺 To my Mom & all the hard-working mothers this Mother's Day...
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/105455/images/Gabe%20Amo%20Header_V6.png]
Dear Friend,

Growing up, my mother came to Rhode Island from Liberia in search of a better life for her family.

Mom worked tirelessly as a nurse, often taking on double shifts to provide for us.

I remember helping her prepare for her citizenship test when I was eight years old. During that time, Mom would work all day, take care of us, and still find the time and energy to give that test her all. That helped inspire my career in public service.

It is because of my mom's hard work and dedication that I have been able to pursue my dreams and work for former Governor Raimondo (a great Mom herself) and in the White House in two presidential Administrations. Mothers like her make our country what it is.

Click to watch this video Mother's Day card to my mom and all the hard-working mothers and mother figures out there. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/email-mom]

Happy Mother's Day!

Thank you,
Gabe Amo

https://www.facebook.com/gabeamoforcongress [https://www.facebook.com/gabeamoforcongress] https://twitter.com/gabeamo [https://twitter.com/gabeamo] https://www.instagram.com/gabeamoforcongress/ [https://www.instagram.com/gabeamoforcongress/] https://www.gabeamo.com/ [https://www.gabeamo.com/]

Gabe Amo for Congress
P.O. Box 40457
Providence, RI 02940
United States

name: Giffords.org
subject: One year since Buffalo
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
Progress is still possible, but only if our elected leaders step up and take action now.
#

Friends -
One year ago, 10 Black people were murdered by a white nationalist at a Tops Friendly Markets in Buffalo, New York. On this Mothers Day, we’re thinking of all the mothers who went shopping for their families but didn’t make it home, and all of the families who lost a mother one year ago today.
The horror in Buffalo broke our hearts and shocked the nation, but tragically, Buffalo was not an isolated incident. Our country has seen far too many hate-fueled shootings, including the recent mass murder at an outlet mall in Allen, Texas.
Despite all of the darkness, progress is still possible, but only if our elected leaders step up and take action now. In the past year, President Biden signed the _Bipartisan Safer Communities Act_ , the most significant gun violence legislation in a generation.
That was an incredible step forward, but there is still so much more work to do. Congress should increase funding for gun violence research, community violence intervention programs, and the ATF. And above all, Congress must close loopholes in our gun laws and require background checks on ALL gun sales, mandate the safe storage of firearms, and reduce access to assault weapons, bump stocks, and large-capacity magazines.
There are clear steps we can take to save lives, we just need leaders with the courage to act.
We have an opportunity this year and next to elect leaders across the country who are tired of the status quo and are ready to fight back and save lives. But we need your help to make sure we have the resources to support these candidates.
[**If you are able, will you chip in $3 today and help us elect gun safety leaders nationwide who will help lead the charge for protecting our communities and loved ones?**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-may23.Jtluwk&refcode=em230514-1-15&t=1&akid=5614%2E3935704%2EfUbnbE)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Donate $3](https://go.giffords.org/go/17016?c0=giffords-em-may23.hLCBBa&refcode=em230514-1-15&t=20&akid=5614%2E3935704%2EfUbnbE)**

name: Ted Lieu
subject: Standing with VoteVets
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
McCarthy is trying to balance the budget on the backs of our Veterans.
#
Derek, Ted Lieu here.
McCarthy is trying to balance the budget on the backs of our Veterans.
The GOP voted to make severe, dangerous cuts to the Department of Veterans Affairs. These cuts would make it harder for Veterans and Military Families to access the benefits they have earned and depend on.
I joined VoteVets on the Hill after the GOP’s shameful vote to break our commitment to our Veterans because we have to hold them accountable for this vote.
And here’s some good news: VoteVets has a plan to do that.
**[A donation of $5 today, $5 or anything you can afford, will go towards VoteVets’ campaign targeting swing district Republicans who voted to cut billions of dollars in Veterans benefits. And in the long-term fight to take back the House, this could be pivotal. So please, chip in if you can.](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-may23.LTpcsd&refcode=em230514-1-15&t=1&akid=8434%2E3013755%2E7dDJqM)**

name: Katie Porter
subject: Happy Mothers Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
My 3 kids make me a better representative.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=22894%2E3422442%2EbtOu3i)
Derek,
**_There are more men named John than moms with young kids serving in the U.S. Senate.**_
You can help change that. I'm a single mom of three (lightly supervised) public school kids. I'm running for the U.S. Senate because the status quo isn't working for families all across the country. [**I'd be the only single mom of school age kids in the Senate, and I'm ready to fight for the things families need to thrive—like affordable college, childcare, and housing. Can you join us?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230514-1?refcode=EMAH14_1_MOM_20230514_V1_F&refcode2=22894_3422442_btOu3i&akid=22894%2E3422442%2EbtOu3i)
**[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230514-1?refcode=EMAH14_1_MOM_20230514_V1_F&refcode2=22894_3422442_btOu3i&akid=22894%2E3422442%2EbtOu3i)**
As most moms will tell you, my kids inspire my fight for a better future. And this Mother's Day, I'm extra thankful for all three of them—Luke, Paul, and Betsy. They make me a better Congresswoman, and I promise you they're my toughest constituents. For years my son had a sign on his bedroom door that warned me 'zero emissions 2030 or do not enter.' As an oversight champion, I'm pretty proud of their attempts to hold me accountable.
Happy Mother's Day, however you may be celebrating.
Katie Porter

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-footer?refcode=EMAH14_1_MOM_20230514_V1_F&t=2&refcode2=22894_3422442_btOu3i&akid=22894%2E3422442%2EbtOu3i)**
---

* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4s-unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=22894%2E3422442%2EbtOu3i)

name: Fair Fight Action
subject: Meet the Founding Mothers of the voting rights movement:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FAIR FIGHT ACTION
body:
We’re endlessly grateful to these Founding Mothers, who dedicated their lives to fighting for future generations’ freedoms. |
---
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63280822/406224220/-1350807227?amount=20&refcode=bsc_emm_ffa_20230514_f_mothersday_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NjRhNjY5YS02YWYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EaBNApLOUqmpt9TvbNwMaLXAhn4l95v2_EQPcOWMRZs=) You’ve probably heard of the Founding Fathers... but today we’d like to talk about the Founding Mothers of our movement. Let us introduce (or reintroduce) you to a few of these amazing women, whose contributions to the voting rights movement we’re celebrating today: **Mary Church Terrell** was one of the first Black women to earn a college degree in the U.S. In the late 1800s, she banded together with other women’s suffrage and civil rights leaders to form the National Association of Colored Women. After the 19th Amendment was ratified, she continued to fight for Black women’s suffrage and challenge segregation until her death in 1954. **Fannie Lou Hamer** spent her career fighting back against systemic racism present in elections. After facing violence and intimidation as barriers to voting, she helped form the Mississippi Freedom Democratic Party and pushed for greater racial equality in the political process. She was a key organizer of the 1964 Mississippi Freedom Summer and helped found the National Women’s Political Caucus, which remains active today. **Diane Nash** became a renowned organizer during the Civil Rights Movement. She initially got involved in the sit-in movement in Nashville as a student, helped to found the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee, and led the Freedom Riders to challenge segregation. Her role in the 1964 voter registration campaign in Selma, Alabama, led to the passage of the Voting Rights Act of 1965. **Margaret Sloan-Hunter** was a Black feminist, lesbian, and civil rights leader. As a teenager, she worked with the Southern Christian Leadership Conference to march and advocate for voting rights. A former editor of Ms. Magazine and first chairwoman of the National Black Feminist Organization, she was a key advocate in challenging racism and sexism as intersectional oppressions. We’re endlessly grateful to these Founding Mothers, who dedicated their lives to fighting for future generations’ freedoms. And we’re proud to continue that fight today — especially as extremists continue to impose barriers to the voting process. clean_body: We’re endlessly grateful to these Founding Mothers, who dedicated their lives to fighting for future generations’ freedoms. | | | = You’ve probably heard of the Founding Fathers... but today we’d like to talk about the Founding Mothers of our movement. Let us introduce (or reintroduce) you to a few of these amazing women, whose contributions to the voting rights movement we’re celebrating today: Mary Church Terrell was one of the first Black women to earn a college degree in the U.S. In the late 1800s, she banded together with other women’s suffrage and civil rights leaders to form the National Association of Colored Women. After the 19th Amendment was ratified, she continued to fight for Black women’s suffrage and challenge segregation until her death in 1954. Fannie Lou Hamer spent her career fighting back against systemic racism present in elections. After facing violence and intimidation as barriers to voting, she helped form the Mississippi Freedom Democratic Party and pushed for greater racial equality in the political process. She was a key organizer of the 1964 Mississippi Freedom Summer and helped found the National Women’s Political Caucus, which remains active today. Diane Nash became a renowned organizer during the Civil Rights Movement. She initially got involved in the sit-in movement in Nashville as a student, helped to found the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee, and led the Freedom Riders to challenge segregation. Her role in the 1964 voter registration campaign in Selma, Alabama, led to the passage of the Voting Rights Act of 1965. Margaret Sloan-Hunter was a Black feminist, lesbian, and civil rights leader. As a teenager, she worked with the Southern Christian Leadership Conference to march and advocate for voting rights. A former editor of Ms. Magazine and first chairwoman of the National Black Feminist Organization, she was a key advocate in challenging racism and sexism as intersectional oppressions. We’re endlessly grateful to these Founding Mothers, who dedicated their lives to fighting for future generations’ freedoms. And we’re proud to continue that fight today — especially as extremists continue to impose barriers to the voting process. = If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically: | = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- We hope you have a wonderful Mother’s Day, however you’re choosing to celebrate. 💜 The Fair Fight Action Team | * 1270 Caroline Street | Suite D120-430 | Atlanta, GA 30307 ©2023 Fair Fight Action - All rights reserved. name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: Getting out the Gen Z vote is our surest path to sane gun laws. Americans can vote out gun-crazy Republicans and stop these massacres, like voters in every other democracy have done.
body:
This school-shooting-drill generation has had enough
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4031d3cf-3fe4-4f1e-9e85-9538c776b221/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | Peter, **The massacre in Allen, TX last weekend was horrifying & heartbreaking. Those who lost their lives include a five-year old child, just beginning life.** _I'm not gonna go into statistics. I don't have the heart to. So, just three things:_ **1)** [**_We can stop this any time we elect enough Democrats. Democrats support the measures that have ended these tragedies in every other democracy._**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T444cd539-a8a1-42f7-93b8-a921b61d5f42/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) **2) _Because of the GOP, we aren't safe anywhere._** **_Houses of worship. Malls. Schools. Dance clubs. It's only getting worse._** **3) _Gen Z is, sadly, the school-shooting-drill generation._** **_They've had enough of this, and the past two elections it's been a top issue turning them out in record numbers, breaking electoral records._** **_Voters of Tomorrow has been key to turning out Gen Z in such massive numbers they've upended our electoral landscape. Continuing to swell their presence at the polls is our only way to stop this utter madness._** [**_Will you join a heartbroken Blue Amp Action in giving $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can to Voters of Tomorrow?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4127a837-82c6-409d-b434-e56e3863f82b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) **This. Must. End.** Cliff
---

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tc2adb8e7-6420-4578-a0c7-3b6c0cabf95f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Team WFP
subject: We’re willing to wait for our favorite shows. Are you?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party National PAC (77 Sands St
body:
| | | Peter, We’re now on the second week of the writers’ strike, and TV shows across networks and streaming services have halted as workers take a stand against months of unpaid work, meager wages, and replacement by AI. **Here are just a few of the shows on hold while the studios refuse to negotiate:**
* Stranger Things (Netflix)
* Abbott Elementary (Disney)
* Severance (Apple TV)
* Loot (Apple TV)
* Hacks (Warner Bros/Discovery)
* Yellowjackets (Paramount)
* Cobra Kai (Netflix)
* Handmaid’s Tale (Disney)

**The sooner executives making millions off the work of writers come back to the table and treat them fairly, the sooner we’ll get our favorite shows back. But in the meantime, we’re willing to wait.** **[If you’re in this fight with us, click here to let us know which of your favorite shows you’re willing to wait for in order to secure fair treatment for writers.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXdJUvH8Ygu3R43gD0WUPlZ4sEfwZeaQlGh2gN-zACE3tWmiS8fuyP8LyAplb53xyzEJqdQ7iYKfSzqaBdIBo4qtkhiXsVmF0KyaX2gD5VnLtfQ6HmLpyvA98vXIuKL_HPKMFTffqbhRC-C7bJP6jDNtkKX8L-b9uCYTUljbepcCn3gKWNZIY-dSEHGF6XmqWivsJRhIMNzf4PjTxl5abdjZ3czrUUphytzofGxFGGoFDYjLT-Ox37PZu-ck7_l-eiTmAo-HXku16l_WuyKStQzbRYfljEu8A0d234jbA2-5JLuDMzM5fAj5ZUSHb2unO_IllOl13OaBoxiL6AFBqxzuh38tPOpWqP7q-ILUDskyF/3w6/vrjrMSc6SdqMGeHvV27E7g/h0/JhIf5Pid-ApvaYoyM8gi_d3jnrGs4U6eqRfZqmMNXw0)** **[I stand in solidarity](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXdJUvH8Ygu3R43gD0WUPlZ4sEfwZeaQlGh2gN-zACE3tWmiS8fuyP8LyAplb53xyzEJqdQ7iYKfSzqaBdIBo4qtkhiXsVmF0KyaX2gD5VnLtfQ6HmLpyvA98vXIuKL_HPKMFTffqbhRC-C7bJP6jDNumAe6XRnCrdWeXfwCYVAeK5iRh4zZ7jj_Z0gRFFunFx1WM95su0GZn8I7TBl5iXkOZojsijQ7RiXm6R06HaIgaubv0iIqBEvlM7z3L4asb-xgPVSFQH87h-Wd4L_Pd--mU9GMTgTWBQf2_azhYQfVfaSbLJcbV6L2O1msKUTbOcFCuC0GoXjxVfWxrWmaF3RsEU86HEOm-0_MrvAR5cle0/3w6/vrjrMSc6SdqMGeHvV27E7g/h1/LVqAtRN52K9RJeO0ZXnPZwHQh308-E2gjv7PRqWimA8)** In solidarity, Team WFP P.S. clean_body: | | | Peter, We’re now on the second week of the writers’ strike, and TV shows across networks and streaming services have halted as workers take a stand against months of unpaid work, meager wages, and replacement by AI. Here are just a few of the shows on hold while the studios refuse to negotiate: Stranger Things (Netflix) Abbott Elementary (Disney) Severance (Apple TV) Loot (Apple TV) Hacks (Warner Bros/Discovery) Yellowjackets (Paramount) Cobra Kai (Netflix) Handmaid’s Tale (Disney) The sooner executives making millions off the work of writers come back to the table and treat them fairly, the sooner we’ll get our favorite shows back. But in the meantime, we’re willing to wait. In solidarity, Team WFP P.S. Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party National PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Working Families, please .

subject: the wealthiest man in PA is going all-out to stop Helen Gym
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
Peter Willis,

With Our Revolution and Bernie Sanders’
endorsed candidate for the Mayor of Philadelphia within striking distance
of victory, Pennsylvania’s wealthiest man is flooding the airwaves with
attack ads in a desperate attempt to stop Helen Gym from winning.

We expected nothing less. This is what happens when a real progressive
with a background as a teacher and a union organizer gets close to winning
in a city historically controlled by a corrupt political machine.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8599?refcode=EM_NBI_OR_20230514_FR_Philly_0_6MO_OurRevolution_NA_NA_Philly&t=1009&akid=4274%2E5885482%2ENACSfn ]Our Revolution has 40,000 progressive super-voters in Philly that we’re
targeting for voter outreach — and in a race this close, every dollar will
matter. Please donate here to help us close our $10,421 budget gap and
fund voter turnout in the final days!

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to
Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8600?refcode=EM_NBI_OR_20230514_FR_Philly_0_6MO_OurRevolution_NA_NA_Philly&t=1010&akid=4274%2E5885482%2ENACSfn ]CHIP IN $10 NOW » [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8601?refcode=EM_NBI_OR_20230514_FR_Philly_0_6MO_OurRevolution_NA_NA_Philly&t=1011&akid=4274%2E5885482%2ENACSfn ]CHIP IN $18 NOW »
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8602?refcode=EM_NBI_OR_20230514_FR_Philly_0_6MO_OurRevolution_NA_NA_Philly&t=1012&akid=4274%2E5885482%2ENACSfn ]CHIP IN $27 NOW » [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8603?refcode=EM_NBI_OR_20230514_FR_Philly_0_6MO_OurRevolution_NA_NA_Philly&t=1013&akid=4274%2E5885482%2ENACSfn ]CHIP IN $100 NOW »

All of the forces holding cities around the country from serving their
citizens, including police unions, public school privatizers, real estate
developers, and the billionaire class — are pouring everything they have
into stopping Helen from winning, and we need your help to fight back in
the final days.

Just like we saw in Chicago, where progressives won big, this election is
a battle between big money that wants to stop change and grassroots
organizing.

If we all stand together and double down on person-to-person voter
turnout, we’re going to win here, just like in Chicago. That’s why our
work is so critical.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/8599?refcode=EM_NBI_OR_20230514_FR_Philly_0_6MO_OurRevolution_NA_NA_Philly&t=1016&akid=4274%2E5885482%2ENACSfn ]Can we count on you to pitch in now to help us get out the vote in
Philadelphia?

When we organize, we win!

Our Revolution

body:
As the Republican party continues its attempts at a power grab, we must be relentless in our efforts to get them out of office.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63281782/406228888/-342827316?refcode=BLU-VFC-20230514-z-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1Mzk3NTE2MS03MGYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Czag9qR51jTrhSfsG9yqE_A4XSecGnQbEkQQfNegKeM=)

| Peter,

I want to take a moment to remind you what’s at stake for voting rights in North Carolina and across the country.

The new conservative majority in North Carolina’s Supreme Court has _**just struck down a previous ruling that blocked egregious partisan gerrymandering.**_
**This is bad news for our democracy.**

It is not an exaggeration to say that Republicans are seeking to further disenfranchise North Carolina’s Black and Brown voters.

As the Republican party continues its attempts at a power grab ahead of the 2024 election, **we must be relentless in our efforts to get them out of office.**

In Congress, I’m working every day to stand up to Republicans and protect our equal access to the ballot.

[_**Peter, I’m counting on your support to fight back. Anything you can contribute will go a long way to ensure everyone has equal access to the ballot.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63281786/406228889/-342827316?refcode=BLU-VFC-20230514-z-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTc5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1Mzk3NTE2MS03MGYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Czag9qR51jTrhSfsG9yqE_A4XSecGnQbEkQQfNegKeM=)

name: Chrissy
subject: In motherhood and in Congress
body:
| | |
---
Derek, in my time as a Congresswoman – and my decades of being a mother – I've noticed a lot of similarities between these jobs.
In motherhood and in Congress, my “constituents” are always on my mind. My job is never "done" – there's always something else around the corner.
In motherhood and in Congress, I feel the joys of success acutely – and also profoundly feel the deep pain when we fall short of our goals.
And in motherhood and in Congress, I honor and acknowledge those who came before me – those who paved the way.
**This Mother's Day, I hope you can take time to let yourself be celebrated – or to celebrate the mothers or mother figures in our lives.** And if this is a difficult holiday for you, know that my thoughts are always with you, for there are many different types of "families" on this planet.
There is work to be done,

Chrissy

name: =?utf-8?B?RWRkaWUgJiBFSiBUZXLDoW4=?=
subject: To my wife, to the best mom.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Raquel Terán for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/raquelteran2024?refcode=X_050923_Fighter&amount=10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/raquelteran2024?refcode=X_050923_Fighter&amount=10]
Happy Mother’s Day to the most amazing mom that I know, my wife Raquel. I’m reminded each day how lucky I am to have her as my partner, and how lucky our son EJ is to have Raquel as his mom.
To all the moms out there, thank you. For the tremendous work and sacrifices you make, both big and small, for your children and your families.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/raquelteran2024?refcode=X_051423_MothersDay&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/raquelteran2024?refcode=X_051423_MothersDay&amount=25]
Believe it or not, but I first met Raquel when she knocked on my door while canvassing with Mi Familia Vote in my neighborhood. >From that day onward I have always admired and loved her tenacity, her passion and her devotion to her work. She is so deeply committed to advancing justice, fighting for working families and ensuring immigrant communities like ours are heard.
On this Mother’s Day, I want to take this moment to celebrate my amazing wife Raquel and all the moms out there who help make this world a better place.
Much love,
Eddie & EJ Terán

subject: Motherhood should be a CHOICE

Peter, It’s my second Mother’s Day since welcoming my son, Finn, into the world. While being a mother is the greatest gift I could have asked for, it’s also the hardest job in the world. I couldn’t be more grateful that it happened on MY terms. Going through pregnancy or becoming a mother is a CHOICE. One that each woman deserves to make for herself — without the interference of Republican politicians or conservative judges. **So this Mother’s Day, we’re doubling down on our efforts to protect the rights of all women, mothers or not, to make their own decisions about their own bodies. name: Martha Melvoin
subject: Nick’s always wanted to make a difference
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nick Melvoin for Congress - FEC ID #C00831289
body:
|
---
| | **Hi, Peter! It's Martha, reaching out on Mothers' Day to tell you about my son Nick and why I'm so proud to be his mom.** From day one, Nick always wanted to make a difference. Whether it was serving on student council or being the captain of his soccer team or volunteering at Camp Harmony or even just calling his grandparents to say hello or writing thank you notes because he knew they'd make someone feel valued, he always wanted to contribute to making things better. When Nick graduated from elementary school, he actually won the school's "Contribution Award" for all that he did for the school. He was 12! [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63282817/406232460/-524197044?refcode=2023-05-14-mothersday&recurring=0&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=746f080f-72f2-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQxNjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzQ2ZjA4MGYtNzJmMi1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ucYR-A6-3onJ8rWu8t3yqJ2LzCk2HOb1GW4ayMSPaYE=) (No surprise to his mother that just a few years later he'd be contributing to every school in Los Angeles.) And now, Nick wants to contribute not just to LA, but to the entire country, by running to succeed another nice Jewish boy, Rep. Adam Schiff, in Congress. **And sure, it's easy to make your mom proud, but watching him lead this campaign brings it to a whole new level. I know he'll work just as hard to make sure the 30th district is proud, too.** **Nick has always contributed to his community and nothing would bring me more joy on this Mother's Day than for you to contribute to him. I hope you'll join me and donate to Nick's campaign today.** [Donate Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63282818/406232461/1183499175?refcode=2023-05-14-mothersday&recurring=0&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=746f080f-72f2-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQxNjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNzQ2ZjA4MGYtNzJmMi1lZDExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ucYR-A6-3onJ8rWu8t3yqJ2LzCk2HOb1GW4ayMSPaYE=) If this email raises $25,000, Nick tells me he'll soon give me the title of **grand** mother, too. (OK, I made that part up, but can you help me encourage him and Kim in the near future?) Nick has so much more to contribute and thanks for your contribution to him today! Happy Mothers' Day, _Martha_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | clean_body: | | | Hi, Peter! It's Martha, reaching out on Mothers' Day to tell you about my son Nick and why I'm so proud to be his mom. From day one, Nick always wanted to make a difference. Whether it was serving on student council or being the captain of his soccer team or volunteering at Camp Harmony or even just calling his grandparents to say hello or writing thank you notes because he knew they'd make someone feel valued, he always wanted to contribute to making things better. When Nick graduated from elementary school, he actually won the school's "Contribution Award" for all that he did for the school. He was 12! = (No surprise to his mother that just a few years later he'd be contributing to every school in Los Angeles.) And now, Nick wants to contribute not just to LA, but to the entire country, by running to succeed another nice Jewish boy, Rep. Adam Schiff, in Congress. And sure, it's easy to make your mom proud, but watching him lead this campaign brings it to a whole new level. I know he'll work just as hard to make sure the 30th district is proud, too. Nick has always contributed to his community and nothing would bring me more joy on this Mother's Day than for you to contribute to him. I hope you'll join me and donate to Nick's campaign today. = If this email raises $25,000, Nick tells me he'll soon give me the title of grand mother, too. (OK, I made that part up, but can you help me encourage him and Kim in the near future?) Nick has so much more to contribute and thanks for your contribution to him today! Happy Mothers' Day, Martha | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | | Paid for by Nick Melvoin for Congress - FEC ID #C00831289 |

In _Shelby County_ , the SCOTUS functionally eliminated the requirement for states with histories of racial discrimination to receive preclearance before passing new election laws. **The results have been far-reaching and devastating** , with GOP lawmakers across the country scrambling to restrict voting access by closing polling places, purging voter rolls, imposing new identification requirements, and more.

Now, experts warn that the Court’s opinion in _Allen v. Milligan_ could pave the way for a similar cascade of racial gerrymandering.
“[A] ruling that sides with Alabama would be a **radical departure from previous interpretations** of the law and would likely free states to draw congressional or state legislative **maps where minority voters have less political power** relative to their numbers.”
_– FiveThirtyEight_

**In other words, Republican-led states could be free to gerrymander with impunity – even if it dilutes the voting power of communities of color.**
| [Donate to help the NDRC fight for fair maps](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63282932/406233145/-644827789?amount=30&refcode=EM_RTI_20230514_FR_5R_redlight922_527&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjE4MmQ1MTc2LTcyZjItZWQxMS05MDdjLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=HdiFmkQpjfcUkn0DV3smgEGnnR1kbvIRsG1BDRGzXE0=)
---
At the NDRC, we’re not sitting around waiting for SCOTUS to rule. Instead, we’re working to safeguard against the worst-case scenario by going state-by-state to fight for fair maps, protect the right to vote, and more.

[**_Will you pitch in any amount you can today to help us keep up this critically important work?_**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63282933/406233146/-644827789?amount=30&refcode=EM_RTI_20230514_FR_5R_redlight922_527&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjE4MmQ1MTc2LTcyZjItZWQxMS05MDdjLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=HdiFmkQpjfcUkn0DV3smgEGnnR1kbvIRsG1BDRGzXE0=)

President Obama said **“The fight to safeguard our democracy is happening every day. That’s how it’s always been, and that’s how it will continue.”** And the way we see it, we’ve got two choices: **1.)** We can give up and let Republicans continue chipping away at democracy piece by piece, or **2.)** We can fight back with everything we’ve got to make sure it survives for future generations.

[**_Please, team, join us in choosing option 2 and make a donation to the NDRC to support our fight to defend our democracy._**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63282934/406233147/-644827789?amount=30&refcode=EM_RTI_20230514_FR_5R_redlight922_527&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjE4MmQ1MTc2LTcyZjItZWQxMS05MDdjLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=HdiFmkQpjfcUkn0DV3smgEGnnR1kbvIRsG1BDRGzXE0=)

clean_body: | Nearly ten years ago, Republican lawmakers successfully convinced the Supreme Court to gut key provisions of the Voting Rights Act. Now, they’re asking the Court to finish the job. | = Team, will you help us fight to defend voting rights? If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = In Shelby County , the SCOTUS functionally eliminated the requirement for states with histories of racial discrimination to receive preclearance before passing new election laws. The results have been far-reaching and devastating , with GOP lawmakers across the country scrambling to restrict voting access by closing polling places, purging voter rolls, imposing new identification requirements, and more. Now, experts warn that the Court’s opinion in Allen v. Milligan could pave the way for a similar cascade of racial gerrymandering. “[A] ruling that sides with Alabama would be a radical departure from previous interpretations of the law and would likely free states to draw congressional or state legislative maps where minority voters have less political power relative to their numbers.” – FiveThirtyEight In other words, Republican-led states could be free to gerrymander with impunity – even if it dilutes the voting power of communities of color. | = At the NDRC, we’re not sitting around waiting for SCOTUS to rule. Instead, we’re working to safeguard against the worst-case scenario by going state-by-state to fight for fair maps, protect the right to vote, and more. = President Obama said “The fight to safeguard our democracy is happening every day. That’s how it’s always been, and that’s how it will continue.” And the way we see it, we’ve got two choices: 1.) We can give up and let Republicans continue chipping away at democracy piece by piece, or 2.) We can fight back with everything we’ve got to make sure it survives for future generations. = – The NDRC = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. We know we send a lot of emails, but we think it's important to keep you updated with the latest information on Republican gerrymandering and voter suppression and how we're fighting back -- regardless of whether you choose to donate. = to make a donation. Every dollar you give will go toward our fight to end gerrymandering and voter suppression. | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 1015 18th Street NW, Suite 1000 Washington, DC 20036 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC.

Josh Hawley is obsessed with controlling our lives.

| Peter,

Josh Hawley thinks the only way to be a man is to be just like him — **and this month, he’s even publishing a whole book about it:** [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63283385/406234879/-146036273?amount=5&amounts=10,25,50,100&refcode=UTS_20230514_EMSPL_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5NDc2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjRmNzhmNjhmLTc0ZjItZWQxMS05MDdjLTAwMjI0ODMyZWI3MyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=-h4adLjmGmeNCrG5HwwD_0jl0wcPnH-6ws7URJLqVsI=) This guy wants to control what happens in your bedroom, in your doctor’s office, and what you do with your own body. name: Dr. Ben Carson
subject: What are your thoughts?
body:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/vWLVb-cDNS

ACI - Religious Liberty Survey

I am fearful that legal persecution will intensify
against Americans holding to faith-based values

http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/oWLVb-cDNS

Fellow
American,

There is
a very concerning trend occurring in our country that has me
worried for Americans who hold to biblical & faith-based values.

After the
Respect for Marriage Act was passed by a bi-partisan effort and
signed into law, I am fearful that legal persecution will only
intensify against Americans holding to faith-based values and who
wish to conduct their lives according to their faith.

Are you
concerned that America is abandoning a basic tenet of our
founding-Religious Liberty?

In
addition to the passing of the Respect for Marriage Act, numerous
examples over the past decade have concerned me: KENNEDY v.
BREMERTON SCHOOL DISTRICT, MASTERPIECE CAKESHOP v. COLORADO CIVIL
RIGHTS COMMISSION, and 303 CREATIVE v. ELENIS are just a few
examples of legal persecution against Americans holding to
faith-based values who wish to conduct their lives according to
their faith.

Furthermore, an amendment to the Respect for Marriage Act,
brought forth by Senator Mike Lee, prohibiting the Federal
Government from retaliating against schools, businesses, and
organizations because of their religious beliefs about same-sex
marriage failed.

This has
the potential to only intensify legal persecution against
individuals, businesses, churches, and nonprofits holding to
faith-based values.

The
American Cornerstone Institute is a non-profit that I founded to
preserve and promote the cornerstones that laid the foundation
for the greatest nation the world has ever seen: Faith, Liberty,
Community, and Life.

Whether
it is a football coach losing his job for praying at midfield
after a game, or a baker being sued for not wanting to bake a
cake that violates his religious values, are you concerned as I
am with the increase in legal persecution against Americans who
wish to conduct their lives according to their faith?

Please
share your thoughts in this quick 2-minute survey concerning
religious liberty in America.
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/61797235:WLVb-cDNS:m:3:2532013465:E9B4624664321ECD24077F18F78191D4:r

Thank you
for your consideration.

Regards,

Dr. Ben
Carson
Founder &
Chairman American Cornerstone Institute

Your
donation to ACI helps us to focus on our mission of promoting and
preserving individual and religious liberty, helping our
country's most vulnerable find new hope, and developing methods
to decrease the federal government's role in society and to
improve efficiency to best serve ALL our nation's citizens.

Copyright
© 2023 American Cornerstone Institute, All rights reserved.

This message was intended for: dpwillis67@gmail.com
You were added to the system January 18, 2022.
For more information please follow the URL below:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/iWLVb-cDNS

http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/oWLVb-cDNS

with "Unsubscribe" as the subject line.

body:

**
Jim Jordan is doing Trump’s dirty work in Congress — _disgusting!_

He’ll do whatever he can to DESTROY President Biden and his powerful Democratic agenda... _so we’re making a HUGE call to Democrats everywhere._

[50,000 SIGNATURES NEEDED: Sign our EMERGENCY petition to show President Biden we stand with him »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/Stand_with_President_Biden/?source=VPP_20230514_EMPET_E2_T0_AC_RS&t=6&akid=7683%2E878179%2EQqPr5e) [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/Stand_with_President_Biden?refcode=VPP_20230514_EMPET_E2_T0_AC_RS&t=7&akid=7683%2E878179%2EQqPr5e) | [**SIGN NOW: STAND WITH PRESIDENT BIDEN AGAINST REPUBLICANS’ BASELESS INVESTIGATION »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/Stand_with_President_Biden/?source=VPP_20230514_EMPET_E2_T0_AC_RS&t=8&akid=7683%2E878179%2EQqPr5e)
---
Jim Jordan is DESPICABLE.

body:
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63284073/406238200/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2OWJmMjFiZS03OGYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kXo8ws5SwCKhtpTouqTT6MX-luhseXvI5ICQEvjZdJk=)
Hi Team —
When Robert decided to run for Congress in 2021, he also made one critical decision that continues to affect how our campaign runs to this day.
He decided to reject all corporate PAC money.
That was a really big decision because campaigns these days are very expensive, especially in the state of California. And with the amount of dark money fueling MAGA campaigns recently, it was a risky decision. But it was a good one.
Robert is very proud that our movement is funded largely by grassroots donations to emails just like this one.
But that also means that during key moments and times when we need help, we have to reach out to you first.
**If everyone who is reading this email contributes whatever they can, we’ll easily hit our midmonth goal. We know not everyone can, but that makes each donation that much more important.**
So as we approach our crucial midmonth fundraising deadline tomorrow at midnight, we are asking:
[**Can you please make a $3 contribution, or anything you can afford, to Robert’s campaign so we can continue fueling our campaign for California families? Every donation will put us one step closer to reaching our goals.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63284074/406238210/1270846539?refcode2=69bf21be-78f2-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&refcode=em230514-1-t1a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2OWJmMjFiZS03OGYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kXo8ws5SwCKhtpTouqTT6MX-luhseXvI5ICQEvjZdJk=)
**[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63284075/406238238/1270846539?refcode2=69bf21be-78f2-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&refcode=em230514-1-t1a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2OWJmMjFiZS03OGYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kXo8ws5SwCKhtpTouqTT6MX-luhseXvI5ICQEvjZdJk=)**
So thank you in advance for contributing whatever you are able to give.
Together,
Team Robert Garcia

subject: My mom: “Do your best. Do what’s right. And never step on anyone else to get ahead.”

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63284331/406238725/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NWJmMjFiZS03OGYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=h25Yq1MTbtTfQMDbNlj9osdMmJ2BPf1zQvu7SW3mTXM=&emci=37bcf9c6-f8f0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=65bf21be-78f2-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=7731215)

| | This is the first Mother's Day of my life with my mother not here with us to celebrate, but she's still with me as I ponder the best way to move our country forward and her edict: _Do your best. Do what's right. And never step on anyone else to get ahead._ I greatly appreciate your ongoing support as I fight for a better foundation for working families in health care, housing, education, and good paying jobs, pursue rapid action to combat climate chaos, defend our democracy, and ensure full equality for the LGBTQ+ community. Below, I've attached a personal note that I wrote about my mother when she passed away earlier this year. All my best, _Jeff_
* * *
**Reflections on the Life of Betty Lou Collins Merkley** My mother, Betty Lou Collins Merkley, passed away early this morning. Here's a little more about her life: As she wrote in an introduction to a collection of her life stories: "I have lived a full life, blessed with my wonderful husband Darrell, and the joy of raising two children and the opportunity to share in the lives of their children and grandchildren." Betty was born into hard times in rural Kansas. When her dad, Thomas Collins met her mom, Leona Cullison, he was a widow. His first wife Daisy had died in 1918 at age 31, leaving him six children to raise. Her mom, Leona Cullison, had been abandoned by her first husband, leaving her with three children to raise. She was living in a train car and surviving by hand washing clothing for other families when the county took away her children. Thomas and Leona had four children together, of which Betty was the oldest. When she was seven, her mother Leona died in an operation. Shortly thereafter her dad moved the family to rural Arizona outside Clay Springs where a son from his first family had a property where they could dry farm and live. From that challenging start Betty had a good childhood. She recalled moving into town in Clay Springs and then Pinedale with a life full of community ballgames, camping, family adventures, and school activities. There was no money, but they lived simply and Betty recalled that they never went to bed cold or hungry. She met Darrell, the love of her life, when he rolled into Pinedale to visit his family. He offered her a ride on his motorcycle and the rest is history. They married on Christmas Eve of her senior year of high school in 1944 and had 62 wonderful years together. They had many adventures together in their early married years as Darrell moved between contract logging and jobs as a union mechanic. In 1948 they decided to visit family that had moved to Oregon. They loved southern Oregon and decided to stay, finding jobs at Harbor Plywood's veneer mill in Riddle: Darrell on the green chain before becoming a millwright and Betty in the office. So long to Arizona and the heat in Mesa and Phoenix! They built a home on Mill Creek; were flooded out; bought a home in Myrtle Creek; made many great friends; and loved the community dance club. They would have stayed in Myrtle Creek forever but the mill closed, so they moved with the timber economy to Roseburg and then east Multnomah County. One thing was missing from their lives. They deeply desired children. While waiting for the stork to deliver they hosted foster children and explored adopting children. But the stork finally arrived, bringing first my sister Linda and then me. We could not have asked for better parents. Betty prepared us for school with math flash cards and nightly book reading, hosted campfire girls and boy scout patrol meetings, and led the local PTA. She loved to garden, watch tennis, play pinochle, track genealogy, and read novels. Together the family enjoyed camping, sailing, and racing quarter midget cars. In later life, Betty and Darrell played a huge role in helping raise their grandchildren: Linda and John's children Erin, Lauren, and Stefan; and Jeff and Mary's children Jonathan and Brynne. Mom's philosophy for her children was: Do your best. Do what's right. And never step on anyone else to get ahead. She would often say, just follow the Golden Rule to treat others as you would want others to treat you. Betty was the last alive among her twelve sisters and brothers. She is survived by her children, her grandchildren, and five great grandchildren. |
---|---|---

name: Anna Kaplan
subject: I'm going to defeat George Santos
disclaimer_text: Paid for by AB PAC
body:
| | |
---
Congressman George Santos claimed he was Jewish.

He claimed his grandparents survived the Holocaust and that his mother survived the September 11th terrorist attacks in New York City.

He claimed he graduated from college as a star volleyball player, worked for Goldman Sachs, and ran a foundation that rescued 2500 dogs and cats.

**_These claims were lies – all of them. _**

And that doesn’t even scratch the surface of the web of brazen lies Santos told to get elected.

**George Santos is a disgrace. He’s an embarrassment to our district, state, and country. And now, he’s facing 13 federal charges for crimes including money laundering and fraud.**

name: Ian Silverii
subject: Let me tell you about an incredible Colorado mom
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brittany Pettersen for Colorado
body:
Somehow, she does it all, week after week.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63285399/406242093/-4707655?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDIyOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNmNkNzcyNy03YWYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bSBn7-fptc0QrAMb6EIjLNRdYzaW6GVdCiMC-YnjJK4=&emci=656acd29-daf0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=36cd7727-7af2-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=19954364) ****Hi there, This Mother’s Day, I’d like to take a moment to tell you about my wife and my best friend, Brittany Pettersen (who also happens to be my Congresswoman). It’s not easy being a full-time member of the United States House of Representatives with a packed schedule while also being an incredible, dedicated mom to our three-year-old son, Davis. But somehow, Brittany does it all, week after week. Of course, we’re talking about the same person who won one tough campaign after another to fight for our values despite Republicans hitting her with every vicious attack they could think up (and plenty they just made up). She’s the person I met almost 14 years ago while she was flagging down strangers in the freezing cold to raise money for a nonprofit she believed in. (I called my mom right after and told her I’d met the girl I was going to marry. I was right, I like being right.) She’s the person who put herself through school doing everything from working a cash register to cleaning houses, and overcame one obstacle after another to get where she is today. And yet, every day, she still manages to astonish me as she fearlessly fights for what she believes in and delivers real progress for the community she loves. **From fighting for the right to choose to standing up for our democracy to working to tackle our nation’s opioid epidemic — Brittany does so much for all of us, and I was hoping that, as one of Brittany’s grassroots supporters, you could join me in showing her that we see and appreciate all her work.** **[So friend, can I count on you to add your name to Brittany’s Mother’s Day Card thanking her for all she does?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63285400/406242094/61344263?refcode=email_051423_mothersday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDIyOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNmNkNzcyNy03YWYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bSBn7-fptc0QrAMb6EIjLNRdYzaW6GVdCiMC-YnjJK4=&emci=656acd29-daf0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=36cd7727-7af2-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=19954364)** | | [SIGN THE CARD →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63285401/406242095/61344263?refcode=email_051423_mothersday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDIyOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzNmNkNzcyNy03YWYyLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bSBn7-fptc0QrAMb6EIjLNRdYzaW6GVdCiMC-YnjJK4=&emci=656acd29-daf0-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=36cd7727-7af2-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=19954364)
---
Thank you for helping out here, Team. I wish you and your family a happy Mother’s Day!
Sincerely,
Ian Silverii

name: Katie Britt
subject: Happy Mother’s Day, Peter!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Britt for Alabama, Inc
body:
| | |
---
[](http://links.katiebrittforsenate.com/u/click?_t=64058844820c43dab0e6ed55204bc487&_m=d826f0f16cf34fa7a2cc7749ab32d83f&_e=u9SMY-0RW63AKkb6PA6JZP_t8f_tRz2DAm0NkjjZL007C9UKuUDnyVrPG2Uu6RgTIqOjEUsQmXRVTNtLnvH7v1ZrQvjzrr-5tEEuuZa8Vmtlb0L2WWlVwgcNNavh3a4FbIjWrRy0GA4_kwh-HSnZN5wIUtf24UInlTB1Qjod34s3wmx1iJrMlpcZYOG3fILhoRAZJ6sbEG9W9QEgHiioDqQn29BPegHTe-hzs1zHpzmUcLvHFAcmT35CLbpzk4P0wl_JL7Ks446Efwiv4aS4jV2RBr8BVPgCdYqAQQ6goUmZzFK_BlbNXR_TeODFOQnyxErBNiZLcqEut8rxFB2PyfgB8R7hADaOowKO-H__73BaTESNUStSK0CMf-DP9o5IvJJvqkbZk5JoAHJE-WlFkUBCjnr5ygMz9JXv6ZwBP4X0uAYDyGe_jWAMWN_zUQAc)
---
Peter, Throughout our lives, our mothers have always been there for us. They’ve selflessly given everything they had and they’ve loved us unconditionally. That’s why I want to honor all of Alabama’s incredible moms today. Thank you for always showing up and showing love. For never taking a day off. For never loving half-heartedly. For always being there when needed. We all owe you a debt of gratitude. Alabama’s mothers deserve the world, and I’m honored and humbled to serve them in the Senate. I’ll never forget the hundreds of thousands of moms across our state and the hard work they all do. I’ll always fight for Alabama’s moms and I’ll make sure their voice is heard when making decisions that impact their children. I am so grateful for everything that Alabama’s moms do for the next generation of Alabamians. Thank you from the bottom of my heart for the work, love, and effort that you put in every single day. [**I would love to know, Peter, how has your mom impacted your life?**](http://links.katiebrittforsenate.com/u/click?_t=64058844820c43dab0e6ed55204bc487&_m=d826f0f16cf34fa7a2cc7749ab32d83f&_e=u9SMY-0RW63AKkb6PA6JZP_t8f_tRz2DAm0NkjjZL007C9UKuUDnyVrPG2Uu6RgTIqOjEUsQmXRVTNtLnvH7v1ZrQvjzrr-5tEEuuZa8Vmtlb0L2WWlVwgcNNavh3a4FbIjWrRy0GA4_kwh-HSnZN5wIUtf24UInlTB1Qjod34s3wmx1iJrMlpcZYOG3fILhoRAZJ6sbEG9W9QEgHiioDqQn29BPegHTe-hzs1zHpzmUcLvHFAcmT35CLbpzk4P0wl_JL7Ks446Efwiv4aS4jYwOmd2k4GkSC_xf0WU1mWUnnXlSFV0iZtRdFd_DnPfWVBKa9PZlYvzs2tZR9PmoyBBT1q3nXNzLJ53104knpikBcs8u4nVBL8RKI2nlmTMKU2OZPC3nZAOkik7rfNdU_WwhwlDVY9b99kHq1wmzBBzGbzY7dx24Z8XPnN3DDSe_) Sincerely, Senator Katie Britt

clean_body: | | | Peter, Throughout our lives, our mothers have always been there for us. They’ve selflessly given everything they had and they’ve loved us unconditionally. That’s why I want to honor all of Alabama’s incredible moms today. Thank you for always showing up and showing love. For never taking a day off. For never loving half-heartedly. For always being there when needed. We all owe you a debt of gratitude. Alabama’s mothers deserve the world, and I’m honored and humbled to serve them in the Senate. I’ll never forget the hundreds of thousands of moms across our state and the hard work they all do. I’ll always fight for Alabama’s moms and I’ll make sure their voice is heard when making decisions that impact their children. I am so grateful for everything that Alabama’s moms do for the next generation of Alabamians. Thank you from the bottom of my heart for the work, love, and effort that you put in every single day. Sincerely, Senator Katie Britt | | Paid for by Britt for Alabama, Inc.

Peter, Everything I do, I do as a Mother. I'm not just running for Congress as a military veteran and concerned citizen, I'm running as a mom, to protect my kids and our country's youths. This Mother's Day I want to give a sincere thanks to our nation's mothers, the ones who make sacrifices every day to make sure our kids grow up safe and secure, with strong morals and values, and a sense of duty and patriotism. I'm so thankful to my mom for **raising me right**. I truly believe there is no better way to set up your kids for success, than by instilling good conservative values of respect, personal responsibility, and the courage to pursue your dreams. Living in Joe Biden's America, I'm truly worried for the future of our children. Between the economy, social issues, and the rise in crime, the Biden administration is tearing our country apart. I'm just one Mom trying to make a difference, but with your help, I feel strongly that we can protect our children and set them up for a future of success. [_**On this Mother's Day, please contribute $6 or more and help me kick off my campaign so I can fight for the future of our kids!**_](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=270ad825d56a49ba87f38e53284ca32a&_m=c3e21eff47a74beba5ea7835812b555b&_e=ZlXyMmy2pMRDx7AwHlF-8Vk3gyQBCtmIcFwgJCJ7aB3u6Bo_i7JjivOi70iEFxG-qASBBoXQYHM6eZV6r198pA1S_p5D2paYIaiANBFGrhszjhrsi46tqJPK9CXsLdO7eBWPH0J2rWCstEJ88ce8wbK85ArsG5LZz9WCpBxjYP0rwsW9mdYjWQazk3gahTW5z9egxVSAKO6a7XxjoVi23UNL7IZKYT5qAo--AmXNIX0eNXJXlDu5Q3ntlIGM0BNFM8RwmeWVZlVKI8_eul3b7w%3D%3D) Thank you... and Happy Mother's Day!**Mariela Roca**

name: Biden-Harris HQ
subject: Celebrating all of the moms and mother figures in our lives
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
Today, we’re celebrating all of the moms and mother figures in our lives. | | |
---

_If this holiday hits you hard, our hearts go out to you._
Peter -- Today, we’re celebrating all of the moms and mother figures in our lives -- those who care for us, sacrifice for us, encourage us, and love us. Happy Mother’s Day! Joe and Kamala’s mothers had a profound impact on their lives, and the lessons they passed on stay with the president and vice president to this day: _“My mom was an incredible woman, and I miss her. She would always tell us that 'courage is the greatest virtue, because without courage you cannot love with abandon.' She lived her life that way, loving us with abandon.”_ **\-- President Joe Biden**

_Even as [my mother] taught us to keep our family at the center of our world, she also pushed us to see a world beyond ourselves. She taught us to be conscious and compassionate about the struggles of all people.”_ **\-- Vice President Kamala Harris**

Joe, Kamala, and our entire team are so grateful for our moms and all of the incredible mothers who have supported us throughout our lives. Once again, happy Mother’s Day. \-- Biden-Harris HQ

|

Every dollar we raise right now will make a monumental difference in shaping the future of states like Pennsylvania.
Grassroots donations have been instrumental in fueling our Democratic success. Together, we’ve achieved remarkable milestones. From expanding health care access to protecting voting rights, Democrats are fighting tirelessly to build a fairer, more inclusive America for all.
Here's how your donation can create an immediate impact:
1. **GOTV Efforts:** Your contribution could enable our party to reach thousands of voters, reminding them of critical elections and urging them to exercise their right to vote. We have the chance to build a better future by ensuring Democrats turn out in force.
2. **Targeted Advertising:** By investing in strategic advertising campaigns, we can effectively communicate our messages and get voters off the sidelines. Gifts from grassroots supporters like you will help Democrats reach every corner of Pennsylvania and across the nation.
3. **Grassroots Organizing:** Our volunteers and organizers are the backbone of our movement. With your support, we can provide them with the necessary resources, training, and infrastructure to engage voters, organize events, and foster meaningful connections in states like Pennsylvania.

[Join Gov. Shapiro and Sen. Fetterman in supporting the DLCC's work by donating $7 to the DLCC before Tuesday's must-win special elections.](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953zawayJU7NskUpAoU2FWeYrplTq_6v4_7gQisubd1A2bMoZLZNP4_5F6Qt_-lLpnolhHicnh-pWcLVg8QSkfGt1xtXlI9dw-RWntpZwBxIXikUPlJdTIifZhOAXUG5XISn246KNO9KuOwLz3kZ1dMdPAuhkuhkmEu4k1u9VXLWZpWzpopUnUbatgpqr-yy0uml__ZkB0Zf1Qv5Nljincc1qalW8uexBZ0XaGiWl3717esfCyX9FT7WlwTKfap97t_eiJQ7mbfWd45kpcRlA47E7R0jMdLK6bamO42zcX9j40tl0vqCu-9E27jVe3SHut5fX3v4DRdOClTnqgUQjHRauUtU1eXUHtAHBM7rXKyOB6dAuBoexBOoLTepunc6i-3TPsjE3aQ5ubR87mPZj4Q2TKLpHF4eC4Gp77vQYeG-XxQ/3w6/HC4r1JTPQFiOzAsXuhjeTg/h6/wCRehuChIk1S4HeaSsTdxsLvARv0woceD4xtMG2ZDLY) **If Democrats lose both races in Pennsylvania, we'll be forced to hand control back to the GOP – and we can't jeopardize our hard work or allow them to jam through an extreme agenda.**
Thank you so much,
DLCC Team

name: Andy Beshear
subject: Celebrating mothers everywhere! 🌸
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ANDY BESHEAR FOR GOVERNOR
body:
None of us would be here without our mothers and the sacrifices they made to bring us into the world. Peter,

I want to take a moment to wish you and your loved ones a Happy Mother's Day!

Being a mom is both very challenging and very rewarding, and it is a job that deserves our respect and support. My wife, Britainy, is an all-star mother in more ways than I can count. She shows me daily how lucky I am to have her by my side, raising Will and Lila (and Winnie!) and using her time and energy to support kids across Kentucky with boundless love and kindness. |
---
As governor, I am proud to fight for programs that support mothers, children, and families across our Commonwealth. Since day one, I've been committed to investing in affordable healthcare and childcare, quality public education, economic development that will lead to prosperity for the next generation, early childhood education, and policies that allow mothers and parents to provide the best possible opportunities for their children.

I’m also grateful for my own mother, Jane, for raising me with the strong Kentucky values of faith, family, and hard work. Every day, I’m so proud of all the work she’s done to impact lives across Kentucky — as First Lady, as an advocate and as a leader for the Commonwealth.
---
To all the mothers: Thank you for the sacrifices you make every day to ensure your children have a bright future. Thank you for the love and support you give your families, even in the toughest of times. Thank you for the positive impact you have on our communities and our world.

Happy Mother's Day to all the amazing moms out there.

Sincerely,

Andy

clean_body: We're seeing evidence of huge amounts of leftwing money flowing into TX... | | | Fellow Conservative, This is urgent. I am asking for help. The left is pouring MILLIONS into Texas to try to take me down. It's only been a few days since far-left Dem Colin Allred announced he's running against me in 2024. But the massive Democrat fundraising machine is already churning out millions. There is a massive wave of cash flooding into his campaign in the 36 hours since he's announced. He's raised over $2 million in a day and a half. That is indicative of the tidal wave of cash that is going to come in- and we 're just getting started. The message is clear - Democrat donors are hellbent on flipping Texas Blue next year, and they'll spend whatever it takes to make it happen. Please send an emergency donation today to help me FIGHT BACK and Keep Texas Red! The corporate, mainstream media is already running cover for Allred. They're describing him with words like "moderate," "bipartisan," and "middle-of-the-road." But here is the truth: Look, if you're a leftist in Manhattan, San Francisco or Chicago, you're giving money to this guy, they all are... Why? Because Allred is a hardcore Democrat who voted with Pelosi 100% of the time. He voted for 87,000 new IRS agents to audit middle-income Americans. He voted for Biden 's assault on the U.S. oil and gas industry. He repeatedly voted for open borders. Colin Allred is too extreme for Texas, and I need your support RIGHT NOW to stop him. Thanks for your help at this crucial moment. For Liberty, Ted Cruz | | | Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate | | 815 A Brazos, PMB 550, Austin, TX 78701 | | | | Defend the Constitution 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314 | _

name: Shamaine Daniels
subject: They're watching us.
body:
www.danielsforcongress.org [www.danielsforcongress.org]
This Mother's Day, remember, our children are watching what we do
Friend
This Mother's Day, I've been thinking a lot about what it means to be a mom in our increasingly complex society. My daughter is in the development stage, and I know that she is quietly absorbing everything I do.
Recently, at a City Council meeting I had a disagreement with one of my colleagues about my daughter's presence at the meeting. For him, this was a matter of childcare. For me it was so much more.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sdemail2023 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sdemail2023]
As a young girl my Dad took me everywhere. That experience prepared me to be a great litigator because it made me comfortable negotiating with those more powerful than me. I take me daughter as many places as I can because I want her to grow up to be comfortable in public spaces, and all spaces.
I want my daughter to be a woman who is comfortable participating in the discourse of her community. I want her to have the confidence to stand-up for herself, for her values, and for others. I want her to know that she has valuable contributions to make, and I want her to be empowered to make them.
That is what I am modeling for my daughter by serving on City Council, and by running for Congress.
When we stay silent in the face of oppression, our children notice. When we give in to the politics of hate, and extremism and elect leaders who refuse to build a fairer, more compassionate, and more equitable society they notice.
But when we stand-up and fight for our beliefs, and for those with no voice, when we take action to build the kind of world we want them to grow up in, they notice that too.
This Mother's Day, I'm asking all of us to think about what we are demonstrating to our children about our values, about the people we want to empower them to be, and about the country we want them to live in. I promise, they are watching.
To make a contribution to support our campaign to challenge extremism, and fight for a better, fairer and more compassionate future for our children, click here. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sdemail2023]
Happy Mother's Day,
Shamaine
P.S. If you missed my announcement video, you can check it out below.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lQXcyDlej8Y [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lQXcyDlej8Y]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sdemail2023]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/99379/images/PaidForByDanielsForCongressB.jpg]
Daniels For Congress
P.O. Box 60654
Harrisburg, PA 17106

Peter,
This Mother's Day, I wanted to take a moment to express my heartfelt gratitude and appreciation for all the wonderful mothers out there who have dedicated their lives to nurturing and caring for their children.

Mothers are the bedrock of our families and communities, and they play an essential role in shaping the future of our society. They inspire us with their selflessness, their unwavering love, and their tireless efforts to make the world a better place.

As we celebrate this day, let us take a moment to honor and recognize the hard work and sacrifices that mothers make each day. Whether it's waking up early to prepare breakfast, staying up late to finish work, or simply being there to listen and support us when we need it the most, mothers always go above and beyond for their children.

As a mother myself, I know firsthand the joys and challenges that come with this role. It is a privilege to be a mother, and I am grateful for the opportunity to be a part of my children's lives.

To all the wonderful mothers out there, I want to wish you a very happy Mother's Day. May your day be filled with joy, love, and the warmth of your loved ones.

Sincerely,
